question.text: Under Section 13(2) of the PMLA, who has the power to impose a fine on banking companies or intermediaries for failing to maintain records or furnish information?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 13(2) of the PMLA, the Director, FIU-IND may impose a fine on any banking company, financial institution or intermediary for failing to comply with obligations of maintenance of records or furnishing information. **The Trap:** While SEBI and RBI regulate intermediaries, the PMLA specifically empowers the Director of FIU-IND for AML-related penalties. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is tested in NISM Series III-A and is critical for compliance officers dealing with AML requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Director, FIU-IND has the same powers as a civil court under CPC 1908 for the purposes of Section 13.
